about.heading: About Cadence Keys
about.p1: Welcome! I'm Cadence, your friendly, neighborhood romance writer.
about.p2: By day, I look like an average frazzled mom of two toddlers who rarely does her hair (a mom bun totally counts, right?). By night, I let my imagination fly and write steamy scenes that have taken several of my closest friends by major surprise. I love writing heartfelt stories with relatable characters and a guaranteed happily ever after.
about.p3: Thank you for visiting, and I hope you enjoy getting to know the players of Clark Fork University hockey as much as I do!
about.p4: Find out more about my books at <1>cadencekeys.com</1>

rules.heading: Community Rules
rules.subtitle: Keep it civil and keep it fun
rules.rule1Content: Treat other users with basic human decency. No harassment, personal attacks, or targeting individuals. Disagreeing with someone's opinion is fine — being rude about it is not.
rules.rule2Content: Rate My Performance (RMP) is about sports romance, hockey, and the CFU series. Off-topic posts may be removed. If you are not sure if something fits, ask yourself: "Would someone here care about this?"
rules.rule3Content: Do not use this space as your personal billboard. Excessive promotion, repetitive posts, or obvious spam will be removed. Share content because it is interesting, not because you are selling something.
rules.rule4Content: No illegal content, doxxing, threats, or anything that could put people at risk. This includes sharing personal information about yourself or others without consent.
rules.rule5Content: When discussing books or series plot points, be considerate of others who have not read them yet. Mark your spoilers clearly or keep major plot reveals vague in titles and previews.
rules.rule6Content: If someone breaks these rules, report them instead of responding in kind. Fighting fire with fire just creates more fire, and we are trying to keep things cool here.
rules.rule7Content: Do not post copyrighted material without permission. Fan discussion and fair use commentary are welcome, but do not paste entire chapters of books or share pirated content. See our User Agreement for more details.
rules.enforcementP1: Rule violations may result in comment removal, temporary restrictions, or permanent bans depending on severity and frequency. We are not looking to ban people — we just want everyone to have a good time.
rules.enforcementP2: Moderators have discretion in how these rules are applied. If you have questions or concerns about moderation decisions, you can contact us directly.
rules.enforcementP3: For full details on content policies and your responsibilities, see our User Agreement.
rules.footerP1: Questions? These rules might seem like common sense, but we have learned that spelling things out helps everyone stay on the same page. When in doubt, just be kind.

privacy.heading: Privacy Policy
privacy.whoWeAreContent: Rate My Performance (RMP) is a fan community site focused on sports romance books and the CFU hockey series. This privacy policy explains what information we collect, how we use it, and what rights you have.
privacy.collectIntro: We collect information you provide directly (like when you comment or take quizzes), basic usage data (like which pages you visit), and standard web information (like your IP address and browser type).
privacy.collectItem1: <0>Comments and replies:</0> Display name, comment text, and avatar selection you choose when posting. No email or real name is required.
privacy.collectItem2: <0>Voting and reporting:</0> When you vote on a comment or report content, we record which comment was acted on and use a device identifier to prevent duplicate actions.
privacy.collectItem3: <0>IP address (hashed):</0> When you submit a report, your IP address is irreversibly hashed (one-way encrypted) and stored to prevent abuse. We cannot recover your original IP from this hash.
privacy.collectItem4: <0>Device identifier:</0> We store a randomly generated identifier in your browser's local storage (labeled `rmp_user_id`) to track votes and reports per device. This is not linked to your real identity.
privacy.collectItem5: <0>Usage data:</0> Pages visited, time on site, browser type, device type, and screen resolution — collected through Google Analytics (see Third-Party Services below).
privacy.collectItem6: <0>Error data:</0> If something breaks while you are using the site, technical details about the error (not your personal data) are sent to our error monitoring service (see Third-Party Services below).
privacy.collectClosing: We do not collect sensitive personal information, track you across other websites, or require you to create accounts.
privacy.useIntro: We use the information we collect to:
privacy.useItem1: Display your comments and quiz results
privacy.useItem2: Prevent spam, abuse, and duplicate voting
privacy.useItem3: Moderate content to keep the community safe (including automated content review — see Content Moderation below)
privacy.useItem4: Understand how people use the site so we can improve it
privacy.useClosing: We do not sell your data to third parties or use it for purposes you would not expect.
privacy.legalBasisIntro: If you are in the European Economic Area (EEA) or UK, we process your data under these legal bases:
privacy.legalBasisItem1: <0>Legitimate interest:</0> Running the site, preventing abuse, moderating content, and fixing errors. We have balanced these interests against your rights and believe they are reasonable for a community discussion site.
privacy.legalBasisItem2: <0>Consent:</0> Analytics tracking through Google Analytics. You can withdraw consent by using browser privacy settings or extensions that block analytics scripts.
privacy.cookiesIntro: We do not use traditional cookies for tracking. Instead, we use browser local storage for one item:
privacy.cookiesItem1: <0>`rmp_user_id`</0> — A randomly generated device identifier stored in your browser. Used to prevent duplicate votes and reports. Persists until you clear your browser data. Not sent to any third party.
privacy.cookiesClosing: Third-party services we use (Google Analytics, Sentry) may set their own cookies. See the Third-Party Services section below for details.
privacy.thirdPartyIntro: We use the following external services. Each has its own privacy policy governing how they handle data:
privacy.thirdPartyItem1: <0>Google Analytics</0> — Collects anonymous usage statistics (pages visited, device type, session duration). Data is retained for 26 months by default.
privacy.thirdPartyItem2: <0>Sentry</0> — Error monitoring service that receives technical error data (stack traces, browser info) when something breaks. Does not receive your comments, votes, or personal data.
privacy.thirdPartyItem3: <0>Google Gemini API</0> — Comment text is sent to Google's AI service for automated content moderation (see Content Moderation below).
privacy.thirdPartyItem4: <0>Affiliate links</0> — Our sidebar contains book affiliate links. Clicking these takes you to external sites with their own tracking and privacy practices.
privacy.thirdPartyClosing: We choose reputable services, but we cannot control their data practices. If you are concerned about third-party tracking, consider using privacy-focused browser extensions or settings.
privacy.moderationP1: When you submit a comment, the text may be sent to Google's Gemini AI service for automated moderation scoring. This helps us identify harmful content quickly. The AI evaluates the text and returns a safety score — it does not store your comment or use it for training purposes beyond what Google's standard API terms allow.
privacy.moderationP2: Comments may also be checked against a keyword filter before reaching the AI. Content flagged by either system may be automatically hidden pending review.
privacy.retentionItem1: <0>Comments, votes, and reports:</0> Stored indefinitely as part of the community discussion record. We may delete or anonymize content that violates our rules.
privacy.retentionItem2: <0>IP hashes (reports):</0> Stored indefinitely for abuse prevention. Cannot be reversed to recover your IP address.
privacy.retentionItem3: <0>Google Analytics data:</0> Retained for 26 months (Google's default), then automatically deleted.
privacy.retentionItem4: <0>Sentry error data:</0> Retained per Sentry's default retention period (90 days), then automatically deleted.
privacy.retentionItem5: <0>Local storage identifier:</0> Persists in your browser until you clear your browser data.
privacy.transferContent: Our site is hosted in the United States. Data sent to Google Analytics, Google Gemini API, and Sentry may be processed in the United States or other countries where these providers operate. If you are located outside the United States, your information may be transferred to and processed in countries with different data protection laws.
privacy.securityP1: We take reasonable steps to protect your information, including irreversible hashing of IP addresses, input sanitization to prevent injection attacks, and rate limiting to prevent abuse. No internet transmission is 100% secure, and we do not store sensitive information we do not need.
privacy.securityP2: If we discover a security incident that affects user data, we will disclose what happened and what we are doing about it.
privacy.rightsIntro: Depending on where you live, you may have certain rights regarding your data:
privacy.rightsEveryoneItem1: You can stop using the site at any time.
privacy.rightsEveryoneItem2: You can clear your browser's local storage to remove the device identifier we store.
privacy.rightsEveryoneItem3: You can use browser settings or extensions to block Google Analytics tracking.
privacy.rightsEveryoneItem4: You can contact us to ask what information we hold that may be associated with you.
privacy.rightsCcpaItem1: <0>Right to know:</0> You can request what personal information we have collected about you.
privacy.rightsCcpaItem2: <0>Right to delete:</0> You can request deletion of your personal information.
privacy.rightsCcpaItem3: <0>Right to opt out of sale:</0> We do not sell personal information. We do not share personal information for cross-context behavioral advertising.
privacy.rightsCcpaItem4: <0>Non-discrimination:</0> We will not treat you differently for exercising your privacy rights.
privacy.rightsGdprItem1: <0>Right of access:</0> You can request a copy of the personal data we hold about you.
privacy.rightsGdprItem2: <0>Right to erasure:</0> You can request deletion of your personal data.
privacy.rightsGdprItem3: <0>Right to rectification:</0> You can request correction of inaccurate data.
privacy.rightsGdprItem4: <0>Right to restrict processing:</0> You can request that we limit how we use your data.
privacy.rightsGdprItem5: <0>Right to data portability:</0> You can request your data in a machine-readable format.
privacy.rightsGdprItem6: <0>Right to object:</0> You can object to processing based on legitimate interest.
privacy.rightsGdprItem7: <0>Right to withdraw consent:</0> Where processing is based on consent (analytics), you can withdraw at any time.
privacy.rightsGdprItem8: <0>Right to lodge a complaint:</0> You have the right to complain to your local data protection authority.
privacy.rightsClosing: To exercise any of these rights, contact us at the email below. Because this site is anonymous, we may need to verify your request relates to specific data before we can act on it.
privacy.childrenContent: This site is not directed at children under 16 years of age. We do not knowingly collect personal information from children under 16. If you believe a child under 16 has provided us with personal information, please contact us and we will delete it promptly.
privacy.dntContent: Some browsers send a "Do Not Track" (DNT) signal. There is currently no industry standard for how websites should respond to DNT signals. We do not currently alter our data collection practices based on DNT signals, but you can use browser extensions to block analytics tracking if you prefer.
privacy.changesContent: We may update this privacy policy occasionally. When we do, we will update the effective date at the top and note significant changes here. Continued use of the site after changes means you accept the updated policy.
privacy.footerP1: If you have privacy-related questions or concerns, you can reach out at <1>{{email}}</1>. We are real humans and will try to give you real answers.

terms.heading: User Agreement
terms.welcomeContent: By using Rate My Performance (RMP), you are agreeing to these terms. If you do not agree with them, that is fine — you just cannot use the site. These terms might change occasionally, so check back if you are curious about updates.
terms.whatContent: This is a fan community site focused on sports romance books, particularly the CFU hockey series. We provide discussion space, quizzes, and related content. We are not affiliated with any publisher, sports league, or educational institution.
terms.ageContent: You must be at least 16 years old to use this site. If you are under the age of 16 and located in the European Economic Area or UK, you may need parental consent to use this site under applicable law. By using the site, you confirm that you meet the applicable age requirement.
terms.behaviorP1: You are responsible for what you post and how you behave here. Do not share other people's personal information, do not harass anyone, and do not post illegal content. Basic human decency applies.
terms.behaviorP2: We can remove content or restrict your access if you break our Community Rules or do things that make the site worse for everyone else. We would rather not, but sometimes it is necessary.
terms.contentP1: You keep ownership of what you post, but you give us a non-exclusive, royalty-free, worldwide license to display it, moderate it, and use it as needed to operate this community. We will not sell your posts or use them for purposes unrelated to operating this site.
terms.contentP2: Do not post copyrighted material without permission. Fan discussion and fair use commentary are fine, but do not paste entire chapters of books or share pirated content.
terms.dmcaIntro: We respect the intellectual property rights of others. If you believe that content on this site infringes your copyright, you may submit a takedown request by emailing us at {{email}} with the following information:
terms.dmcaItem1: A description of the copyrighted work you believe has been infringed
terms.dmcaItem2: The URL or location of the infringing content on our site
terms.dmcaItem4: A statement that you believe in good faith the use is not authorized by the copyright owner
terms.dmcaItem5: A statement, under penalty of perjury, that the information in your notice is accurate and that you are the copyright owner or authorized to act on their behalf
terms.dmcaClosing: We will review valid takedown requests and remove infringing content where appropriate. Repeat infringers may have their access restricted.
terms.ourContentContent: The site design, original quizzes, and our original content belong to us. You can use the site as intended, but do not copy our stuff to create competing sites or commercial products.
terms.disclaimersP1: We provide this site "as is" without guarantees of any kind, either express or implied. Sometimes it might be slow, sometimes features might not work perfectly, and sometimes we might have outages. We will try to keep things running smoothly, but we cannot promise perfection.
terms.disclaimersP2: The quiz results are for entertainment purposes only. Your CFU boyfriend match does not constitute relationship advice or guarantee compatibility with fictional characters.
terms.privacyContent: Check our Privacy Policy for details about data collection and use. The short version: we collect what we need to run the site and we are not in the business of selling user data.
terms.terminationContent: You can stop using the site at any time. We can also restrict or terminate your access if you violate these terms or make the site worse for other users. If we shut down the site entirely, we will try to give reasonable notice.
terms.governingP1: These terms are governed by the laws of the State of Montana, United States, without regard to conflict of law principles. If part of these terms turns out to be unenforceable, the rest still applies.
terms.governingP2: If you have a dispute about these terms or the site, please contact us directly first. We will try to resolve it informally within 30 days. If we cannot resolve it informally, any legal action must be brought in the courts located in Montana, and you consent to the jurisdiction of those courts.
terms.liabilityContent: To the maximum extent permitted by law, we are not liable for indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages, or any loss of profits or revenues, arising from your use of or inability to use the site. Our total liability for any claim related to the site shall not exceed the amount you paid us in the 12 months preceding the claim (which, since this site is free, would be zero).
terms.questionsP1: These terms cover the essential legal bases, but they are not meant to be adversarial. We want people to enjoy using this site, and legal documents are just part of running a community responsibly.
terms.questionsP2: If something seems unclear or if you have concerns about these terms, feel free to reach out. We are happy to explain our thinking or address reasonable concerns.

accessibility.heading: Accessibility
accessibility.subtitle: Making this site usable for everyone
accessibility.commitmentContent: We want Rate My Performance (RMP) to be accessible to everyone, including people who use screen readers, keyboard navigation, or other assistive technologies. We aim to meet WCAG 2.1 Level AA standards — the widely accepted benchmark for web accessibility. While we are not perfect, we are actively working to improve the experience for all users.
accessibility.navItem1: Keyboard navigation support for all interactive elements
accessibility.navItem2: Skip link to jump directly to main content (visible when focused)
accessibility.navItem3: Consistent navigation structure across all pages
accessibility.navItem4: Clear, visible focus indicators (outline) on buttons, links, and form inputs
accessibility.navItem5: Proper heading hierarchy (h1, h2, h3) for logical page structure
accessibility.navItem6: Keyboard-accessible modals with Escape key to close
accessibility.navItem7: Focus trapping inside modals so Tab/Shift+Tab stays within the dialog
accessibility.navItem8: Focus restored to the triggering button when a modal closes
accessibility.navItem9: Logical Tab order following reading order (top to bottom, left to right)
accessibility.navItem10: No keyboard traps: users can navigate away from any element using keyboard alone
accessibility.navItem11: All interactive elements have minimum 44×44px touch target size
accessibility.textItem1: Readable sans-serif fonts with sufficient line height
accessibility.textItem2: Dark text (#333) on light backgrounds meets WCAG AA color contrast (7:1 ratio)
accessibility.textItem3: Text scales properly with browser zoom up to 200%
accessibility.textItem4: Alt text on all images and icons that convey meaning
accessibility.formItem1: All form inputs have visible, associated labels or aria-labels
accessibility.formItem2: Form validation errors are clearly identified with descriptive messages
accessibility.formItem3: Error messages explain how to fix the problem (e.g., "Display name must be 30 characters or less. Current: 45")
accessibility.formItem4: Errors are announced to screen readers via aria-live="assertive"
accessibility.formItem6: Critical actions (like reporting comments) require user confirmation before proceeding
accessibility.screenReaderItem1: ARIA labels on buttons to describe their function (e.g., "Like this comment")
accessibility.screenReaderItem2: ARIA alerts for error messages with role="alert" and aria-live="assertive"
accessibility.screenReaderItem3: ARIA status regions for vote count announcements
accessibility.screenReaderItem4: Toggle states communicated via aria-pressed (vote buttons) and aria-expanded (reply threads)
accessibility.screenReaderItem5: Proper form structure with fieldset/legend for grouped controls (avatar selector)
accessibility.screenReaderItem6: aria-describedby and aria-invalid attributes on form fields
accessibility.screenReaderItem7: Dialog and modal windows with proper aria-labelledby and aria-describedby
accessibility.screenReaderItem8: Decorative icons hidden from screen readers with aria-hidden to prevent duplicate announcements
accessibility.screenReaderItem9: Quiz progress bar with ARIA role="progressbar" and live updates for each question
accessibility.screenReaderItem10: Quiz answer buttons with descriptive aria-labels
accessibility.screenReaderItem11: Quiz results announced via live region (role="status" aria-live="polite")
accessibility.mobileItem1: Touch-friendly button sizes (minimum 44x44px) via invisible touch target overlays
accessibility.mobileItem2: Responsive design that adapts to mobile, tablet, and desktop screens
accessibility.mobileItem3: Hamburger menu for mobile navigation with aria-label
accessibility.mobileItem4: Simplified layouts on smaller screens without loss of functionality
accessibility.motionItem1: Respects prefers-reduced-motion: all animations and transitions are disabled when the user's system setting requests reduced motion
accessibility.motionItem2: No content depends solely on animation to be understood
accessibility.knownIssuesIntro: We continuously monitor and improve accessibility. If you encounter any issues, please let us know:
accessibility.knownIssuesItem1: <0>Browser/assistive tech combinations:</0> While we test on major browsers and screen readers (NVDA, JAWS, VoiceOver), some niche combinations may have issues
accessibility.knownIssuesItem2: <0>Third-party embeds:</0> Some ads or embedded content may not fully meet WCAG AA standards due to external provider limitations
accessibility.knownIssuesItem3: <0>Future enhancements:</0> We're always looking for ways to improve. Your feedback helps us identify accessibility barriers we may have missed
accessibility.browserIntro: We test and support accessibility with:
accessibility.browserItem1: <0>Browsers:</0> Chrome, Firefox, Safari (desktop and mobile), and Edge (latest versions)
accessibility.browserItem3: <0>Keyboard navigation:</0> Full support for Tab, Enter, Spacebar, and Arrow keys
accessibility.browserItem4: <0>Browser zoom:</0> Responsive layouts at zoom levels from 80% to 200%
accessibility.browserItem5: <0>High contrast mode:</0> Windows High Contrast Mode support
accessibility.browserClosing: If you're using assistive technology and something isn't working as expected, please let us know. Sometimes the issue may be specific to a combination of browser + screen reader + operating system, and we want to help troubleshoot.
accessibility.standardsIntro: We aim to conform to WCAG 2.1 Level AA standards across the site. In plain terms, that means:
accessibility.standardsItem1: Text is easy to read against its background (at least 4.5:1 contrast ratio)
accessibility.standardsItem2: Everything works with a keyboard — no mouse required
accessibility.standardsItem3: Screen readers can understand the page structure and announce content correctly
accessibility.standardsItem4: Form fields have clear labels and helpful error messages
accessibility.standardsItem5: Nothing times out before you have a chance to read or interact with it
accessibility.standardsClosing: WCAG 2.1 Level AA is the widely accepted standard for web accessibility and helps ensure that most users with disabilities can navigate and use the site effectively.
accessibility.helpP1: If you need content in a different format or are having trouble accessing any part of the site, we're happy to help. We can provide information in alternative formats when possible.
accessibility.helpP2: When reporting accessibility issues, it helps if you can tell us:
accessibility.helpItem1: What browser and assistive technology you're using (e.g., NVDA, JAWS, VoiceOver)
accessibility.helpItem2: What page or feature you're trying to use
accessibility.helpItem3: What happened vs. what you expected
accessibility.helpItem4: Whether the issue affects core functionality (e.g., commenting) or secondary features (e.g., animations)
accessibility.thirdPartyP1: Some features on this site (such as ads or embedded content) come from third parties and may not meet the same accessibility standards that we maintain. We do our best to choose accessible third-party providers, but we can't control everything.
accessibility.thirdPartyP2: If third-party content is blocking your access to core site features (commenting, voting, navigation), please contact us and we'll look for alternatives or provide workarounds to ensure you can use the site fully.
accessibility.footerP1: If you encounter accessibility barriers or have feedback, you can reach out at <1>{{email}}</1>. We are real humans and will try to give you real answers.
